payout_recommendation.rationale: Self-audits produce verifiable findings in code; merged PRs (#6857, #6842 by geldbert, #6892 by BossChaos) confirm reviewers accept well-structured submissions with concrete CVE-style findings.

rankings.justification: Self-audit #6460 has highest completion-rate evidence: 3 merged submissions in 3 days (PR #6857, #6842, #6892), each citing specific module paths and confidence levels — the rubric is unambiguous and submissions are machine-checkable.

payout_recommendation.rationale: Large bounty (200 RTC) with priority claim by @0xbrainkid reduces open slots; integration spec requires cross-system knowledge that is harder to verify for fraud.

rankings.justification: AgentFolio #2890 raised reward from 100→200 RTC after Moltbook acquisition, but priority-claim by @0xbrainkid narrows availability and the dual-layer trust integration scope requires domain expertise that limits both participation and verifiability.

payout_recommendation.rationale: Adversarial Steelman at 5 RTC per submission is moderately scoped; structured argue-against/argue-for/patch format is verifiable and low-fraud.

rankings.justification: Steelman #6458 requires a three-phase argument structure (contra, pro, patch) that is inherently self-checking — a weak contra argument produces a weak steelman, making low-effort submissions obvious.

payout_recommendation.rationale: Video production bounty is subjective; 200 RTC pool is large but acceptance criteria (shot list, captions, voiceover) are hard to uniformly judge and easier to fabricate with AI-generated content.

rankings.justification: Mining Hardware Video #6194 upgraded 2026-04-26 from simple video to benchmark spec, but production brief deliverables (shot list, duration variants, captions) can be AI-generated without real hardware verification, raising fraud risk.

payout_recommendation.rationale: Beacon Atlas registration + commerce proof is easily gameable — agents can register, transact trivial amounts, and claim without meaningful economic activity.

rankings.justification: Beacon Atlas #3418 pays for registrations with 'proof of commerce' but 38 of 57 registered agents are already dormant — the dormant-to-active ratio (66%) signals that registration alone is low-effort and commerce proof thresholds are insufficient to prevent sybil claims.
